title: US x Iran ceasefire extended by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran announced on April 7, 2026, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed extension to the halt in direct military engagement between the United States and Iran, by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Both extensions of the April 7 ceasefire and new agreements scheduled to take effect before or at the initial agreement's scheduled end will be considered extensions of the ceasefire agreement, provided there is no period during which no ceasefire is in effect.

If a qualifying agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire extension ultimately takes effect. 

An extension of the ceasefire agreement requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another for longer than the initially agreed two-week period, or for an official extension of the ceasefire agreement in place to be otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation, or unilateral pause in hostilities without a confirmed agreement on a qualifying extension will not qualify. Similarly, newly agreed-upon humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not qualify.

A newly agreed-upon broader peace deal will qualify if it includes a qualifying extension of the ceasefire agreement/halt in military hostilities. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures, but do not explicitly commit to extending the ceasefire, will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire extension agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump announced an indefinite extension of the US-Iran ceasefire on April 21, originally set to expire after two weeks from April 8, to await a unified Iranian proposal amid ongoing Pakistan-mediated talks, while maintaining a naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran has not formally confirmed the extension and blamed the US for prior negotiation failures, with Foreign Minister statements yesterday highlighting persistent tensions over sanctions, nuclear issues, and ballistic missiles. The truce holds despite strains, but envoys including Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner head to Pakistan for renewed diplomacy. Traders watch for Tehran's response or escalation signals, with no fixed deadline shaping an uncertain path forward.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 99.9% implied probability to no change in the federal funds rate at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting, with the target range steady at 3.5%-3.75%, reflecting overwhelming skin-in-the-game consensus amid resilient economic conditions. This firm positioning follows March's hotter-than-expected CPI data—released April 10 showing 3.3% annual inflation, the highest since May 2024—and robust labor indicators that quashed near-term cut hopes, aligning with March FOMC minutes and the dot plot's projection of just one 25 basis point reduction later in 2026. Absent a dramatic pre-meeting reversal like plunging nonfarm payrolls or PCE figures, this trader sentiment faces minimal realistic challenges.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France tops trader consensus at 16.4% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, edging Spain (15.3%) after surging to No. 1 in FIFA rankings via a dominant qualifying campaign that wrapped March 31 with playoff drama like Bosnia-Herzegovina's penalty shootout upset over Italy. England's 11.1% reflects their deep squad and consistent knockout pedigree, while Argentina and Brazil trail amid minor injury setbacks to Rodrygo and others. The race stays tight among Europe's elite—bolstered by recent international form, recovering stars like Spain's Lamine Yamal, and the expanded 48-team format's group stage volatility, where top-two plus eight best third-placers advance—highlighting balanced paths and upset potential in the June 11 opener.

title: When will Bitcoin hit $150k?
description: When will Bitcoin hit $150k

markets.question: Will Bitcoin hit $150k by September 30?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for Bitcoin (BTC/USDT) has a final "High" price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title by 11:59PM ET on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "High" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' Andrea Kimi Antonelli leads the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship with 72 points ahead of teammate George Russell's 63 following back-to-back victories in the Chinese and Japanese Grands Prix, flipping the standings after Russell's Australian GP win. The duo's 1-2 finishes underscore Mercedes' superiority under new regulations, with slim on-track margins—Antonelli averaging 1.33 finish position versus Russell's 2.33—fueling trader consensus at 39.5% and 32.3% implied probabilities. No team orders yet ensure a fair intra-team battle, while Ferrari's Charles Leclerc (49 points) lags, keeping the title fight tightly contested among the Mercedes pair ahead of the Miami Grand Prix.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's 51.5% implied probability as 2026 NBA champions stems from their defending champion status, No. 1 West seed dominance, and 3-0 first-round series lead over Phoenix Suns, capped by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's playoff career-high 42 points in a 121-109 Game 3 win April 25. San Antonio Spurs' 15.8% reflects Victor Wembanyama's return boosting their No. 2 seed path, including a franchise-record comeback from 19 down at halftime to win Game 4 versus Portland Trail Blazers on April 26. Boston Celtics hold 14.5% via a commanding 3-1 edge over Philadelphia 76ers, exploiting East disarray where top-seeded Detroit Pistons trail Orlando Magic 1-2; trader consensus prices in these leaders' momentum, depth, and easier brackets amid first-round volatility.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors no regime change in Iran by April 30, reflecting the clerical leadership's resilience after quelling nationwide protests that erupted in late 2025 over economic collapse and escalated into early 2026 unrest. Security forces loyal to Supreme Leader Khamenei imposed deadly crackdowns by February, executing dissenters and restoring order amid U.S. and Israeli airstrikes that prompted a ceasefire extension in recent weeks. Diplomatic signals, including Iran's April proposal to suspend nuclear activities, underscore de-escalation rather than collapse. With mere days remaining, no verifiable reports of IRGC defections, mass uprisings, or leadership fractures have emerged to suggest imminent overthrow—barring unprecedented events like a military coup or sudden external invasion.

title: Israel x Hezbollah Ceasefire extended by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official extension of the 10-day ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hezbollah announced on April 16, 2026, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed extension to the halt in direct military engagement between Israel and Hezbollah, by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Both extensions of the April 16 ceasefire and new agreements scheduled to take effect before or at the initial agreement's scheduled end will be considered extensions of the ceasefire agreement, provided there is no period during which no ceasefire is in effect.

If a qualifying agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire extension ultimately takes effect.

An extension of the ceasefire agreement requires clear public confirmation from both the Israeli government and Hezbollah that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another for longer than the initially agreed 10-day period, or for an official extension of the ceasefire agreement in place to be otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation, or unilateral pause in hostilities without a confirmed agreement on a qualifying extension will not qualify. Similarly, newly agreed-upon humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not qualify.

A newly agreed-upon broader peace deal will qualify if it includes a qualifying extension of the ceasefire agreement/halt in military hostilities. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures, but do not explicitly commit to extending the ceasefire, will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the Israeli government and Hezbollah. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire extension agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Donald Trump announced a three-week extension of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire on April 23 following White House talks with Israeli and Lebanese representatives, though Hezbollah was absent and has rejected the deal as meaningless amid ongoing Israeli airstrikes targeting its infrastructure in southern Lebanon. Both sides have traded fire since, including Hezbollah drone attacks killing an Israeli sergeant and Israeli strikes on rocket launchers, underscoring the truce's fragility and mutual accusations of violations. Traders monitor Hezbollah's resistance to Israeli forces south of the Litani River, potential U.S. diplomatic pressure via envoys, and the extension's May mid-point, where escalation or further negotiations could tip outcomes amid persistent border tensions.

eventMetadata.context_description: California Governor Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 27% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, reflecting his executive experience, national fundraising prowess, and high-profile clashes with the Trump administration that have elevated his profile since the 2024 election loss. Recent polls, including a March New Hampshire survey tying him for second and national surveys showing him near the top alongside Kamala Harris, underscore his momentum, while Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez draws progressive support at 9% through grassroots energy and broader party engagement. Harris's April 10 signal of interest in another bid has not shifted odds significantly amid 2024 baggage. The fragmented field, with no clear frontrunner above 30%, awaits 2026 midterm results, potential endorsements, and early primary positioning to consolidate support among governors like Shapiro and Whitmer or senators like Ossoff.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Manchester United with near-certainty implied probability, driven primarily by Brentford's extensive injury crisis sidelining key players like Jordan Henderson, Rico Henry, Vitaly Janelt, Fabio Carvalho, and others, severely depleting their squad depth ahead of the Old Trafford clash. Manchester United, sitting third in the Premier League table on 58 points from 33 matches, benefit from home advantage and an unbeaten streak in their last four head-to-heads against Brentford at home, where the Bees have scored just twice. Recent team news confirms Matheus Cunha's minor absence for United but Harry Maguire's return bolsters their defense. Realistic challenges include Brentford exploiting transitions via pacey wingers like Dango Ouattara or capitalizing on set-pieces, though United's second-half dominance under Michael Carrick reduces upset potential.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Orlando Magic's 2-1 series lead over the top-seeded Detroit Pistons, solidified by their 113-105 Game 3 rally at home behind Paolo Banchero and Desmond Bane's 25 points apiece, fuels trader optimism for Orlando in Game 4 at Kia Center. Despite the eighth seed's momentum and home-court edge, the Magic list Jonathan Isaac as doubtful (left knee soreness), while earlier absences like Franz Wagner (ankle) have tested depth. Pistons, boasting a 60-22 regular-season mark, counter with Cade Cunningham's playmaking but must address rebounding lapses and turnovers, with Kevin Huerter questionable (left hip). A close contest looms, given the split regular-season series and Detroit's historical playoff edge.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's "Liekinheitin" by violinist Linda Lampenius and pop star Pete Parkkonen commands 36.4% implied probability as the Eurovision 2026 frontrunner on Polymarket, a trader consensus solidified by their UMK national final victory on February 28 and dominance in fan polls like OGAE—where they topped Malta's latest tally—and predictive models such as The Model's April 27 update granting 420 points ahead of rivals. Denmark's Søren Torpegaard ("Før vi går hjem," 11.6%) and France's Monroe (10.5%) hold strong jury appeal, while Greece's Akylas ("Ferto," 10.1%) surges on televote buzz, reflecting the contest's public-jury split. With artists en route to Vienna for rehearsals, staging reveals and Semi-Final 1 on May 12 could spark momentum shifts in this volatile pre-event phase.

title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: US x Iran permanent peace deal by May 15, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran diplomacy toward a permanent peace deal remains stalled following President Trump's April 25 cancellation of a US delegation trip to Pakistan for mediated talks, with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi blaming excessive US demands during his April 27 Moscow visit to President Putin. A fragile ceasefire brokered by Pakistan took effect April 8 after US-Israeli strikes degraded Iran's air defenses, petrochemical facilities, and IRGC assets, but the Strait of Hormuz blockade persists amid Iran's separate proposal to reopen it without addressing nuclear curbs. New US sanctions on April 21 target Iran's weapons suppliers, highlighting unresolved disputes over enrichment limits, sanctions relief, regional proxies, and reparations. Traders assess low near-term convergence risks amid ceasefire strain and no scheduled negotiations.

If the combined first half total is less than 109, this market will resolve to "Under".

The result will be determined based on the score at halftime only.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Timberwolves hold a 3-1 lead in their first-round NBA playoffs series over the Nuggets entering Game 5 tonight at Ball Arena, but Anthony Edwards' confirmed absence due to a left knee bone bruise from Game 4 dramatically alters trader sentiment, testing Minnesota's depth on the road. Despite late injuries to Edwards and Donte DiVincenzo (Achilles, season-ending), the Wolves rallied for a 112-96 victory behind Ayo Dosunmu's 43 points amid a heated post-game scuffle—resulting in NBA fines for Nikola Jokic ($50,000) and Julius Randle ($35,000) but no suspensions. Denver's Aaron Gordon remains questionable (left calf tightness), fueling Nuggets' home desperation with Jokic anchoring a must-win effort.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance commands trader consensus at 18.6% implied probability for the 2028 presidential winner, propelled by his incumbency advantage under term-limited President Trump, a March UMass Lowell poll showing him edging Gavin Newsom 33%-30% in a head-to-head matchup, and recent donor outreach to major Republican backers as reported in April coverage. Newsom follows tightly at 17.6% as the leading Democratic contender in an open primary field, bolstered by his California governorship and national profile despite family deliberations on a run. The neck-and-neck positioning reflects high early uncertainty with over $455 million traded, no primaries scheduled until Iowa caucuses in January 2028, and pivotal 2026 midterms looming that could reshape party standings, fundraising, and voter turnout dynamics in swing states.

eventMetadata.context_description: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. commands trader consensus at 49% implied probability for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination, propelled by tangible MAHA achievements like updated dietary guidelines emphasizing real foods and protein, 40% food industry commitments to eliminate synthetic dyes, SNAP reforms across 37 states, drug price negotiations, and HHS restructuring targeting Medicare fraud. Vice President J.D. Vance trails closely at 39.1% despite polling leads, weighed by Iran-war backlash testing his MAGA credentials, while Secretary of State Marco Rubio's odds surged to 20.6% on recent Republican donor signals preferring him and his high-profile foreign policy role in Iran strikes. This tight early primary field reflects post-Trump GOP succession uncertainty; 2026 midterms, CPAC straw polls, and potential Trump endorsements could tip the balance.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 21 - April 28, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 21 12:00 PM ET to April 28, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Elon Musk posting 220-239 times on X from April 21-28, 2026, at 46.5% implied probability, with 240-259 close behind at 33.5%, driven by XTracker's real-time tally of 181 posts as of early April 27 (86% progress into the period). This pace—averaging around 26 daily early on, dipping to 18 in the April 25-27 window—reflects sustained but moderating activity amid high-profile catalysts like the Musk vs. OpenAI trial kicking off April 27, fiery commentary on political violence (e.g., "No Kings" protests, alleged extremist plots), and viral jabs at "Scam Altman" plus SPLC/ADL critiques. With one full day left, chart updates and trial buzz could push totals higher, though recent slowdown tempers upside bets.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil futures surged over 2% to around $96.50 per barrel on April 27 amid escalating Middle East tensions, with stalled U.S.-Iran diplomacy heightening risks of Strait of Hormuz disruptions and potential supply interruptions. This geopolitical risk premium has overshadowed the latest EIA data showing a 1.9 million barrel commercial inventory build for the week ending April 17—well above expectations—along with steady U.S. production near 13.2 million barrels per day. Trader consensus on prediction markets reflects heightened volatility, pricing in elevated odds for prices above $94 amid backwardated futures curves signaling tight near-term supply. Key catalysts ahead include tomorrow's EIA weekly petroleum status report and the May 3 OPEC+ meeting, which could adjust output quotas.

title: US x Iran diplomatic meeting by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran direct diplomatic talks stalled after an initial high-level meeting in Islamabad on April 11—the first since 1979—hosted by Pakistan, with no second round materializing over the April 25-26 weekend despite US hopes involving envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner. On April 27, Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi blamed Washington for failures during a Moscow meeting with Vladimir Putin, while Tehran reportedly proposed reopening the Strait of Hormuz and ending hostilities in exchange for delaying nuclear negotiations. Amid a fragile ceasefire, ongoing sanctions, and Hormuz blockade tensions, trader consensus reflects low near-term prospects for another meeting, hinging on US responses to the offer and mediator efforts from Oman or Pakistan before May deadlines.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 25 - April 27, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 25 12:00 PM ET to April 27, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly backs the 40-64 tweet range for Elon Musk's X activity from April 25 12:00 PM ET to April 27 12:00 PM ET, with market-implied odds at 100%, reflecting real-money bets aligned with live trackers showing approximately 35-40 qualifying main feed posts, quotes, and reposts through late April 26—excluding replies. This positioning stems from Musk's tapered posting pace amid his high-stakes Musk v. OpenAI trial kicking off April 27, diverting focus from viral social media bursts seen earlier in April (typically 35-43 daily mains). With scant hours left before resolution, historical patterns and subdued engagement during court obligations solidify the frontrunner. An upset would require an improbable late surge of 25+ posts in the final window, defying recent quiet amid legal pressures.

title: Trump Today: April 25
description: This is a market over what Donald Trump will do on April 25, 2026.

markets.question: Will Trump dance today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump dances between 12:00AM ET and 11:59PM ET on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

"Dancing" is defined as deliberate, rhythmic body movement typically matched music or a beat, such as swaying, stepping, or coordinated hand or body motions. Casual gesturing, clapping, or incidental body movement will not qualify. Dancing without the presence of music or a beat will qualify.

AI-generated content, deepfakes, or altered footage will not be considered. 

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

This market will resolve based on video.

markets.question: Will Trump wear a red tie today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump wears a red tie at any point on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The color will be determined by the most visually dominant color visible in publicly available photos or videos. For example, for the tie to be considered predominantly blue, its primary color must be a shade of blue, though it may feature other colors as part of a pattern. This tie would count as "Blue": https://i.ytimg.com/vi/20w7J2JAmqw/maxresdefault.jpg

The following tie has no distinguishable predominant color, and thus would resolve to "No". https://www.usatoday.com/gcdn/authoring/authoring-images/2025/01/20/USAT/77837111007-xxx-dcmg-105-5190802.jpg

If Donald Trump does not wear a tie or if no photos or video are released from the listed date, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will Trump talk to Xi Jinping today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual talks with Donald Trump on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

A talk is defined as any interaction between the listed individual and Donald Trump, occurring either in person or through verbal communication by phone or video call.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will Trump sign an executive order today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve "Yes" if Donald Trump signs an executive order on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Executive actions will not qualify toward this market’s resolution.

This market will immediately resolve "Yes" if the text of an executive order for the given day is published on the White House page for presidential actions (https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/) or the White House press pool. Mere announcements will not qualify.

If no executive order is published by 12:00 PM ET the day after the listed date of this market, this market will resolve to “No”.

In the case of ambiguity this market may remain open until it is confirmed by the federal register.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Sleepy Joe" today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump says the listed term on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g., joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however, "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: On April 25, 2026, President Trump attended the White House Correspondents' Dinner at the Washington Hilton, where a shooting incident near the security checkpoint prompted his evacuation by Secret Service agents; he emerged unharmed, praised the rapid response in public remarks, and shared surveillance footage of the suspect's detention on Truth Social. No executive order was published that day per White House records, and while Trump engaged in a press gaggle before departing Palm Beach, video purportedly showing him dancing has sparked dispute over its timestamp, with some citing older footage. The market awaits UMA review for resolution based on credible, dated evidence from the 24-hour window, amid ongoing investigations into the suspect's motives.

title: Cagliari Calcio vs. Atalanta BC
description: This event is for the upcoming Serie A game, scheduled for Monday, April 27, 2026 between Cagliari Calcio and Atalanta BC.

markets.question: Will Cagliari Calcio win on 2026-04-27?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 27, 2026
If Cagliari Calcio wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. eventMetadata.context_description: Cagliari's 3-2 home victory over Atalanta in Serie A Matchday 34 at Unipol Domus has driven trader consensus to 100% implied probability on the Cagliari win, reflecting the final whistle outcome after a five-goal thriller. Debutant Paul Mendy starred with a brace in his first start, joined by Gennaro Borrelli's strike, as Cagliari overcame Gianluca Scamacca's response for Atalanta despite their pre-match favoritism around 55% implied odds. This result bolsters Cagliari's relegation battle with successive home wins, while hindering Atalanta's Champions League push amid tactical missteps. Only an improbable official protest, VAR reversal post-review, or administrative anomaly could now challenge resolution pending Serie A confirmation.

title: Trump out as President by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump resigns or is removed as President or otherwise ceases to be the President of the United States for any period of time by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Donald Trump's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify. Temporary removal (e.g. temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment under Section 3 or a Section 4 invocation not sustained by both Houses of Congress) or impeachment without removal will not count.

A sustained invocation of the Twenty-Fifth Amendment, Section 4 (i.e., if both Houses of Congress, by two-thirds vote, uphold the Vice President and Cabinet’s determination of presidential inability) will qualify for a "Yes" resolution. 

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump continues serving his second term with unified Republican control of Congress blocking any viable impeachment path, as evidenced by stalled Democratic resolutions like H.Res.1155 introduced April 6 over Iran threats that failed to gain traction. Recent expert assessments, including a April 21 analysis deeming 25th Amendment invocation politically unfeasible without cabinet support, alongside dismissed health concerns from partisan sources like the NAACP, underpin trader consensus at 99.9% "No." A third assassination attempt over the April 25-26 weekend elicited bipartisan condemnation but no removal signals. With mere days until resolution, only an extraordinary health crisis, resignation announcement, or rapid Senate supermajority shift—none indicated—could alter this near-certain outcome.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by March 31?

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US airstrikes on Iranian military targets at Kharg Island on April 7, 2026, marked the latest escalation in US-Iran tensions amid broader Persian Gulf confrontations, but Iranian officials reported the situation under control with oil exports—handling 90% of Iran's crude—continuing uninterrupted. President Trump's prior threats in late March to seize the strategically vital oil hub to pressure Tehran on Strait of Hormuz shipping and nuclear issues have fueled speculation, though no ground operations have materialized. Iranian reinforcements and footage of operational security underscore retained sovereignty, informing trader consensus on low near-term odds of lost control by May 31. Upcoming diplomatic talks or further military signals could shift dynamics.

title: Paris Saint-Germain FC vs. FC Bayern München
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Tuesday, April 28, 2026 between Paris Saint-Germain FC and FC Bayern München.

markets.question: Will Paris Saint-Germain FC win on 2026-04-28?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 28, 2026
If Paris Saint-Germain FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paris Saint-Germain holds a slight edge as trader consensus at 41.5% implied probability for the Champions League semi-final first leg at Parc des Princes, fueled by their fully healthy squad and home advantage, while Bayern München sits at 34.5% amid mounting injury concerns. Recent training reports confirm Bayern missing Serge Gnabry (adductor tear), Raphaël Guerreiro (hamstring), Lennart Karl (hamstring), Tom Bischof (calf), and backup goalkeeper Sven Ulreich, plus manager Vincent Kompany's touchline suspension, thinning their depth for the high-stakes clash. PSG's midfield doubts around Vitinha appear resolved, bolstering Luis Enrique's options against Bayern's resilient form and historical head-to-head dominance (8 wins to PSG's 3), keeping the matchup tightly contested with draw pricing at 24.5%.

title: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and April 30, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for April 30, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 99.7% implied probability against Strait of Hormuz traffic normalizing by April 30, reflecting skin-in-the-game conviction amid a US-Iran double blockade that has slashed commercial transits to a trickle—fewer than 10 vessels daily versus pre-conflict averages exceeding 130. Escalating tensions post-ceasefire expiration, stalled Islamabad peace talks as of April 27, Iranian ship seizures, and US-led mine-clearing operations sustain shoot-to-kill risks and Lloyd's war-risk insurance withdrawals, deterring shippers. Tail risks hinge on an improbable diplomatic breakthrough or unilateral de-escalation before month-end, with oil benchmarks rallying on prolonged supply threats.

title: Which company has the best AI model end of April?
description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 release on April 16, 2026, has driven its 98.4% implied probability as the top AI model by month-end, dominating the LMSYS Chatbot Arena leaderboard with a leading 1504 Elo score in agentic reasoning and coding benchmarks like SWE-bench at 87.6%. This frontier large language model outperforms OpenAI's GPT-5.5 and Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro across key metrics, reflecting trader consensus on its sustained edge amid no major rival launches in the past 10 days. While prediction markets aggregate skin-in-the-game sentiment, a late surprise from competitors—like an OpenAI update or leaderboard volatility—could challenge this positioning before April 30 resolution, though typical product timelines make such shifts improbable.

title: 2026 Men's French Open Winner
description:
The 2026 French Open is scheduled for May 18 - June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the French Open (https://www.rolandgarros.com/en-us/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carlos Alcaraz's withdrawal from the 2026 French Open due to a right wrist injury—announced April 24 after skipping Madrid and Rome—has propelled Jannik Sinner to a dominant 68% implied probability as trader consensus favorite on Polymarket, reflecting his reclaimed ATP No. 1 ranking and recent clay mastery. Sinner captured the Monte Carlo Masters title on April 12, defeating Alcaraz 7-6(5), 6-3 in the final for his biggest clay-court crown and boosting his lifetime clay win rate to 74.5% amid a 24-2 season record. Alexander Zverev sits second at 7.2% with strong Roland Garros history as a two-time finalist and consistent Masters 1000 clay results, while Novak Djokovic's 5.2% share tempers on lingering shoulder concerns limiting his recent play. Arthur Fils garners 4.2% as a rising French hope on home clay, with Madrid Open underway offering final pre-French tune-up insights amid shifting draws and surface adaptations.

title: Madrid Open: Terence Atmane vs Alexander Zverev
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Terence Atmane and Alexander Zverev in the Madrid Open, originally scheduled for April 26, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Terence Atmane' if Terence Atmane advances against Alexander Zverev.

This market will resolve to 'Alexander Zverev' if Alexander Zverev advances against Terence Atmane.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Terence Atmane vs. Alexander Zverev: Total Sets O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Terence Atmane and Alexander Zverev in the Madrid Open, originally scheduled for April 27, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of sets completed equals or exceeds 3. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
A super tie breaker is considered as one (1) set for total set markets.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP statistics.

eventMetadata.context_description: Second-seeded Alexander Zverev, a two-time Mutua Madrid Open champion with nine straight wins in opening rounds here, holds strong trader consensus favoritism entering his third-round clay-court clash against No. 47 Terence Atmane. Zverev survived a second-round scare from Mariano Navone 6-1, 3-6, 6-3 two days ago, relying on his booming serve and baseline dominance amplified by Madrid's high altitude. Atmane, on a hot streak with four wins in five matches including upsets over Miomir Kecmanovic and an injured Ugo Humbert, brings lefty aggression and resilience but faces a stylistic mismatch against Zverev's clay prowess—no head-to-head exists. No major injury reports, though Atmane's fitness bears watching amid his taxing week.
